video_title: Top #DDD Videos in Cleveland with Guy Fieri 🔥 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Fat Gats
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Cleveland to check out a joint that my cousins took my mom to a long time ago. And allegedly, she sent me a message and said, Honey, next time you're in town, you've got to go and check out this joint. The guy's been rocking the place for over 25 years. And it started with money that his dad, who's a doctor, lent to him. This is Fat Gats.

dishes.description: A Filipino rice dish with chicken, Calabrian chilies, cilantro, and a poached egg. Made with simple chicken stock, jasmine rice, garlic, ginger, and fish sauce.
dishes.guy_response: The only problem with the dish is that it'll at some point in time be all gone. It's light, it's bright, it's fresh. Very simple, but great ingredients. Dynamite.

restaurants.name: Mason's Creamery
restaurants.guy_intro: Hey, Murph, if I could offer you scratch-made handmade ice cream or scratch-made handmade ramen, which would you prefer? What if I could offer you both? In the same restaurant? I'd call you crazy. Well, call me crazy. Let's call this place Mason's Ice Creamery.

dishes.description: Ramen featuring char siu pork belly, a rich broth enhanced with mushroom salt, sesame oil, Korean soy sauce, white pepper, and cream cheese, topped with melted raclette cheese, pickled daikon radish, corn, and soy-marinated eggs.
dishes.guy_response: That's outstanding. Not good, not great. Outstanding. It's silky. The addition of the cream cheese is genius. And it works in tandem with the funk of the raclette. I can really taste the caramelization on the pork. It's not supposed to work. But once you have one bite of it, you're like, of course that works. It's completely crazy, but yeah, it's great. Next level, dude.

restaurants.name: Mabel's Barbecue
restaurants.guy_intro: Chef Michael Simon, I love your Cleveland weather. Well, tell me, what's happening? What's new in the world? A little bit of barbecue. And I love barbecue. But do you love Cleveland barbecue? I didn't even know there was such a thing as Cleveland barbecue. Teach me. There is. Mabel's Barbecue, buddy. You're going to love it.

dishes.description: Smoked pork belly with a Cleveland-style barbecue sauce (mustard-based with bourbon, chipotle, Hungarian paprika), poppy seed coleslaw, and a maple and mustard glaze, served on a bun.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. Everything is done in Michael Simon way. Really appreciate the ingredients. Really appreciate the history. You are a real super Cleveland guy. And it comes doing the sandwich. I mean, it is barbecue. It has got its own attitude and its own style. The pork is cooked perfectly. I love this 300 degree pork. You actually got some texture out of the fat. Seasoned properly. The little maple mustard vinegar that came on the end of it was a nice little kiss. And the slaw, crunchy enough, acidic enough. Super balanced. It's really great.

restaurants.name: Astoria Cafe and Market
restaurants.owner_chef: George Kanzios, Steve Daniels and Corey Korinsky
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here. I'm in Cleveland, Ohio. And it's been a while since I've visited. Let me tell you, my cousins remind me of it all the time. You know, the food scene is really blown up here. And with so much culture, when I hear that there's a Greek joint where they're importing their own goods, you know I got to check it out. This is Astoria Cafe and Market.

dishes.dish_name: Veal, Lamb, and Ricotta Cheese Meatballs with House-made Fettuccine
dishes.description: Tender meatballs made from veal, lamb, and ricotta cheese, seasoned with parsley, garlic, salt, pepper, and panko. Fried and finished in the oven, served with a rustic house tomato sauce and fresh fettuccine pasta.
dishes.guy_response: That is without question one of the most tender, fluffiest, lightest flavor-blooming balls I've ever had. A little unique richness that comes in from the lamb. And the pasta is delicious, too. And the tomato sauce is spot on, very nice and rustic. That's what we call a destination meatball. That would go in the Flavortown Hall of Fame. Dude, that is light out.

restaurants.name: The Rally Inn
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Cleveland. Happens to be a joint that's serving Cleveland comfort food. I got to check it out. This is the Rally Inn.

dishes.description: A Cleveland spin on Hawaiian Locomoco, featuring an 8oz ground beef patty mixed with peppers, onions, garlic, and Cajun seasoning. Served with four-cheese pierogis, a rich brown pepper gravy, and fried red onions (frazzles).
dishes.guy_response: This is not the meal plan that the doctor recommended. Nice crust on the patty. Nice little crunchy from the Frizzle McNizzles. The pierogi has got great dough texture to it, nice chew. But the black pepper gravy takes it to another level. Dude, it just lights out.

restaurants.name: Good Company
restaurants.guy_intro: We're in Detroit's Shoreway neighborhood of Cleveland, Ohio, about 500 feet from the lake. A really talented young chef here. He is killing it with like chefed up diner food. Chefed up diner food on Triple D? Perfect. This is good company.

dishes.description: A shaved certified Angus beef tri-tip sandwich on a milk bun, with mushroom duxelles, tarragon chili Bearnaise sauce, and served with a rich lobster beef au jus for dipping.
dishes.guy_response: Whoa. The sandwich is spectacular before you dip it. The beef flavors through the roof. That is crazy. The lobster really comes out. I cannot believe how much lobster flavor's in there. It is full on surf and turf. One of the most whacked out thing I've ever seen on Triple D. Amen, dude.

restaurants.name: Lucky's Cafe
restaurants.guy_intro: I mean, what it's really about is real people making real food, kind of like this joint here in Cleveland, where it's all about farm to table at Lucky's Cafe.

dishes.description: Brisket brined for seven days, then cooked with fresh thyme, carrots, onions, and celery. Served on house-made rye bread with Swiss cheese, house-made sauerkraut, and house-made Thousand Island dressing.
dishes.guy_response: I don't have anything to say. Look at the goosebumps on my arm. That bread, that kraut, with that corn. I don't know what's better on all of this. Now we're going to hit the Thousand Island. Mm. I don't know that you should accept money for these. Bring us your first born. Rich, buttery. It's yummy. All the stuff comes right from the garden outside. Oh, that's great. Isn't that amazing? I love that. What's amazing is you. Absolutely fabulous. I haven't had one item here that I haven't liked.
